narrative: MetraPark is filling up for the playoff game, that particular small-city electricity where the whole town turns out and the parking lot is a social event of its own. Ryan is in the stands with Cody and Leah, Cody vibrating with excitement, Leah pretending she’s above it but watching every play. Cassidy and Dani ended up two rows behind them — Cassidy because she never misses a game, Dani because Cassidy wouldn’t take no for an answer — and the four of them nod at each other in that way people do when they recognize a face but don’t know the name. The gym is loud, the bleachers are packed, and for two hours Billings feels like exactly the right size. Meanwhile Petra is at home, phone pressed to her ear, listening to her senior partner explain that the West End residential project has been moved up. Client review Monday. She hangs up and stands very still in her kitchen for thirty seconds, processing whether this is an opportunity or a trap.
events: The high school basketball playoff game at MetraPark draws a big crowd — Ryan brings his kids, Cassidy and Dani end up in the same section
Petra gets an unexpected call from her firm’s senior partner about a client review on Monday — her West End project is being fast-tracked
